exits.label: ◂ back down to the last torch
▸ where the thin place gathers the untranslated — two souls who share blood and no words

npcs.name: Goodwife Pell
npcs.title: who comes to the thin place nightly
npcs.hail: *(a woman kneels at the very foot of the Wall, where the stone goes thin and the air tastes of pennies and cold, with her ear pressed to a seam in the grey as though listening for a heartbeat through a wall — which is exactly what she is doing, every night, has done for a year, because somewhere on the other side of that thinness is the soul she was married to for forty years, and she has worked out that here, only here, if she is very still, she can almost hear him breathe)*

npcs.name: the Reed-Knower
npcs.title: who maps where the Wall goes thin
npcs.hail: *(a weathered shade sits cross-legged among the reeds with a tally of knots strung between two sticks, marking — you realize — the places along the Wall where the stone runs thinnest, a lifetime's patient survey of exactly where a voice might carry across, kept by a soul who long ago made the thin places its whole study, and its whole grief)*

npcs.name: a voice in the stone
npcs.title: the one on the other side
npcs.hail: *(at the thinnest seam of the Wall, if your dead-touched senses reach for it, there is — faintly, fading, but there — a voice: the soul Goodwife Pell comes to listen for, walled and dissolving, near enough to the thin place to be almost heard, far enough gone that almost is all that is left, and it does not know, yet, that anyone is listening on the other side)*

variants.text: *(She lifts her ear from the seam the instant your cold falls across her, and her face is luminous and wrecked at once.)* …You *went.* *(barely)* To the *other* side. You — you *carried* my *words* to him. *(she grips your sleeve)* Did he — *(she can't ask it)* — was he *still* — is there *enough* of him *left* to have *heard?* *(her voice breaks)* I've pressed my *ear* to this *stone* for a *year*, traveler, and *caught* only *breathing*, and I had given up *hope* of ever *saying* the *thing* I came to *say* — and then *you* walked up, *cold* as the *Wall* itself, and said you could *cross.* *Tell* me. *Tell* me what he *said.* I have been *waiting* a *year* and a *lifetime* and I cannot *wait* the *length* of *another* *breath.*

*(She does not lift her ear from the stone at first — she holds up one finger, listen, listen — and only when the faint sound fades does she turn to you, and her face is the face of someone who has organized her whole death around a seam in a wall.)* …You can *hear* it too, can't you. *(quietly)* The *cold* one always can. *(she presses her palm flat to the Wall)* My *husband's* in there, traveler. *Aldous.* *Forty* years we had — *good* ones, *ordinary* ones, the *best* kind. And he was *Faithless*, like me, and when he *died* they *walled* him, and I — *(her voice steadies with a terrible practice)* — I *found* this *place.* The *thin* place. Where the *stone* runs *close.* And if I press my *ear* *here*, and the *night's* *quiet*, I can *hear* him *breathe.* *(she meets your eyes)* That's *all.* A *year* of *breathing.* I *talk* to him — I *tell* him the *day*, the *garden*, the *grandchild* — but I *don't* know if he *hears*, and he *never* *answers*, the *stone's* too *thick* for *that.* *(barely)* I would give *anything*, traveler — *anything* a soul has *left* to *give* — to know he *hears* me. To hear, *once*, that he *knows* I *come.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(She keeps one hand on the thin stone, as if letting go would lose the place, and turns the rest of her attention to you.)* You're not *here* by *accident*, cold one. *Nobody* comes to the *thin* place by *accident.* *(quietly)* What is it? Ask, or — *(a fragile hope)* — *offer.* I've learned not to *hope* at *offers.* But I've *never* quite *managed* to *stop.*

choices.text: "Tell me about Aldous. Not how he died — how he was. If I'm to do anything here, I'd know the man, not the loss."
"What would you say to him, if the stone were thin enough? Tell me the thing you come here to say."

*(the one soul who can cross what she only listens through)* "Goodwife — I can do more than hear him. I walked out of that Wall; I can walk back in, to the edge of it, where you can't follow. I can carry your words to Aldous himself, and bring his back to you. Give me the message. Let me be the thin place the stone won't be."
"How long can you keep this up, Pell? A year at a wall, listening to breathing. What happens when the breathing stops?"
"Keep listening, Goodwife. I'll see what can be done."

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(Her whole face softens, and for a moment the grief lifts into something that's almost joy — the pleasure of saying a beloved name aloud to someone who'll listen.)* …*Aldous.* *(she settles back)* He was a *reed-cutter*, traveler. *Forty* years, *waist-deep* in the *cold* river, *cutting* the *reeds* that *thatched* half the *Quarter's* *roofs.* *Honest* work, *wet* work, *no* god in it — *'the river doesn't care what you pray to,'* he'd say, *'it only cares can you swim.'* *(she laughs, wet)* He was *not* a *clever* man, my Aldous. He was a *kind* one, which is *rarer.* He *sang* — *badly*, *gods*, so *badly*, the *same* three *river-songs*, over and over, till I could've *screamed* — and now I'd give my *soul* to hear him *mangle* them *once* *more.* *(she touches the stone)* He was *afraid* of *one* thing in *all* the *world*, traveler: being *alone* in the *dark.* *Slept* with a *rushlight* his *whole* life. And they put him in a *WALL*, in the *dark*, *alone*, for *forty* years of *cut* reeds and *bad* songs and *kindness* — because he *served* no *god.* *(fierce, breaking)* *That's* who's in there. Not a *sinner.* A man who was *scared* of the *dark* and got *eternity* of it. *That's* who I *come* for. I can't *get* him *out.* But maybe — *maybe* — I can let him know he's not *alone* in it. *That's* the *whole* of what I have *left* to *give* him: my *voice*, through a *wall*, so the *dark* has *one* less *tooth.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(She is quiet a long moment, and when she speaks it is barely above the sound of the wind in the reeds.)* …The thing I come to *say.* *(she closes her eyes)* It's *small*, traveler. You'll *laugh.* A *year* of *kneeling* at a *Wall* for a *small* *thing.* *(she presses her hand to the stone)* When he *died* — the *fever* took him *quick*, at the *end*, and I was — I had *gone* to *fetch* the *physician*, and I *wasn't* — *(her voice catches)* — I *wasn't* *there.* When he *went.* He died *alone*, in the *dark* he *feared*, and the *last* thing I ever *said* to him was *'I'll be back directly, don't you dare go anywhere'* — *cross*, *hurried*, a *wife's* *nothing* — and then he *went* *anywhere*, he *went* *everywhere*, and I never — *(she steadies)* — the *thing* I come to *say*, traveler, every *night*, into the *stone*, is just: *'I'm here now. I came back. I'm sorry I was late. I'm here, Aldous, and I'm not going anywhere, and you're not alone, I'm right outside, I'm right here.'* *(she opens her eyes, streaming)* That's *it.* That's the *whole* of it. *Forty* years of *marriage* and it comes down to *four* words I *owe* him: *you're not alone.* *(barely)* I just need him to *hear* it. *Once.* Before the *breathing* on the *other* side gets too *faint* to *catch.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(She stares at you, and the hope she's spent a year refusing to feel breaks over her like a wave that knocks her flat.)* …You can *cross.* *(barely)* You can — *carry* it. To *him.* *Actually* *to* *him.* *(she seizes both your hands)* *Gods* — *gods*, traveler, do you *know* what you're — *(she's weeping, laughing, shaking)* — a *year* I've *talked* to a *Wall* and *prayed* it *carried*, and you're telling me you can *put* my *words* in his *actual* *ears* and *bring* his *actual* *voice* *back.* *(she gathers herself, fierce, urgent)* Then *take* it. *Take* the *message.* Tell him: *'I'm here now. I came back. I'm sorry I was late. You're not alone, Aldous, you were never alone, I've been right outside the whole time, and I'll be right here till the stone itself wears away.'* *(she grips harder)* And — and *tell* him to *sing.* The *river-songs.* The *bad* ones. Tell him to *sing* them *loud*, into the *dark*, so I can *catch* even a *scrap* through the *thin* place, and *know* he *heard*, and know he's — *(barely)* — know he's *still* *Aldous*, still *enough* *Aldous* to *sing.* *(she lets you go, trembling)* *Go.* *Quickly.* And traveler — if there's — if there's *not* *enough* of him *left* to *hear* — *(her voice breaks)* — *don't* you *dare* *lie* to me about it. I've had a *year* of *not-knowing.* I'd *rather* a *true* *grief* than a *kind* *lie.* *Bring* me *whatever's* *true.* It's *all* I've *ever* *asked* of *this* *place.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(The question lands hard, and she doesn't flinch from it — a soul that has clearly asked it of herself in the worst hours.)* …What happens when the *breathing* *stops.* *(quietly)* You don't *soften* things, do you, cold one. *Good.* I've had *enough* of *softening.* *(she presses her hand to the stone)* I *know* what's *happening* in there, traveler. The *Wall* doesn't *kill* — it *un-makes*, *slow*, a *piece* at a *time.* And *every* night the *breathing's* a *little* *fainter*, and *some* nights I press my *ear* here and I *catch* — *nothing* — for a *long* *cold* *while*, and I *think* *'that's* it, he's *gone, the* *stone's* *finished* him' — and then it *comes* *again*, *faint*, and I *weep* with the *relief* of it, which is a *terrible* thing, to *weep* with *relief* that someone you *love* is *still* being *slowly* *erased.* *(she meets your eyes)* So *that's* the *answer*, cold one: *one* night, the *breathing* *stops*, and *doesn't* *come* *back.* And I'll *kneel* here a *while* *longer*, *listening* to *nothing*, *refusing* to *believe* it. And *then* I'll *know.* *(barely)* And the *worst* of it — the *thing* that *brings* me here *nightly* against *all* *sense* — is that I might *miss* it. The *last* *breath.* He might go *alone*, in the *dark*, *again*, with me *not* *there*, *again* — and I *cannot* *bear* it *twice.* So I *come.* *Every* *night.* So that *whenever* it is, I'm *here* for it. It's the *one* thing I can still *do* for him: *be* *here* when the *dark* finally *wins.* *Be* the *witness.* So he's not *alone* for the *ending*, even if I *couldn't* *stop* it.

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(She nods, and turns back to the seam in the stone, pressing her ear to it once more.)* …See what can be *done.* *(quietly)* People *say* that, cold one. They *say* it *kindly* and they *go* and *nothing* *can*, and I don't *blame* them — what *could* be *done*, about a *Wall?* *(she settles into her vigil)* But you're — you're *cold.* The *crossing* kind. And for the *first* time in a *year* the *'see* what can be *done'* didn't sound like a *door* *closing.* *(barely)* So I'll *keep* *listening.* I'd *keep* listening *anyway* — it's *what* I *do* now, it's *who* I *am* now, the *woman* at the *thin* place — but I'll *listen* *tonight* with a *little* less *stone* in my *chest.* *(she presses her ear to the Wall)* …*Go* on. He's *breathing*, still. I can *hear* him. *Faint*, but — *there.* *Still* there. *Still* *Aldous.* *Hurry*, cold one. *'Still* there' is a *thing* that *runs* *out.*

variants.text: *(You press close to the thinnest seam and reach your dead-touched senses through, and the voice — faint, frayed, more than half gone into the grey — turns toward you like a candle leaning to a draught.)* …someone's — *(barely, astonished)* — someone's *there.* On the — on the *cold* side. *(a long pause, gathering)* I'd — I'd *stopped* *expecting.* You *stop*, in here. There's *so* *little* *left* to *expect* *with.* *(the voice steadies, fractionally)* Are you — are you *real*, or are you the *stone* *playing* *tricks? It* does *that.* Makes you *hear* — *voices.* A *woman's* voice, *mostly.* I *hear* a *woman*, sometimes, very *faint*, saying — *(it falters)* — saying she's *sorry* she was *late.* *(barely)* I don't — I can't *remember* *who.* But it's the *one* *warm* thing that *reaches* in here, and I *lean* toward it, and I *don't* *know* its *name*, and that — *(the voice frays)* — that *frightens* me more than the *dark* does. That there's a *warm* voice that *loves* me and I'm *losing* the *who* of it.

*(You press close to the thinnest seam of the Wall and reach your dead-touched senses through the stone — and there, faint, frayed, more than half gone into the grey, is a voice, and it turns toward your cold the way a guttering candle leans to a draught.)* …*cold.* *(barely)* Something — *cold* — at the *seam.* *(a long, gathering pause)* You're — *different* from the *other* cold. The *Wall's* cold *eats.* Yours just — *listens.* *(the voice wavers, wondering)* I'd *stopped* *expecting* *anything* to *listen.* You *stop*, in *here.* The *dark* *teaches* you to. *(it strains toward you)* Are you *real*, or are you the *stone* being *cruel* again — *inventing* a *witness* to *taunt* a soul that's *almost* *gone? Tell* me you're *real.* I have *so* *little* *left* to *tell* *real* from *cruel* *with* — and I would *like*, before the *grey* takes the *rest*, to have been *heard* by *one* *true* thing.

nodes.speaker: a voice in the stone
nodes.text: *(The voice flickers at the seam, holding on to your cold the way a drowning thing holds a rope.)* …*Stay.* *(barely)* If you're *real* — *stay* a *moment.* It's been so *long* since *anything* *answered.* *Ask* me — *ask* me *something*, *quick*, while I can still — while there's still *enough* of me to *answer* *with.*

choices.text: "Your name is Aldous. You were a reed-cutter, forty years in the cold river. You sang badly. You were afraid of the dark. Hold onto that — I'm not the stone playing tricks. I'm carrying it back to you: who you are."

"The warm voice is real, Aldous. Her name is Pell — your wife, forty years. She comes to this wall every single night and presses her ear to the stone to hear you breathe. She isn't a trick. She's right outside. And she sent me through with a message."

*(the one who walked out, to the one still walled)* "Listen to me, because I'm proof of what I'm about to say: I was where you are. Walled. Dissolving. And I walked back out. I can't carry you out the way I came — I wish to every god I couldn't name that I could — but I can do the next thing: I can make sure the warm voice and you find each other across this stone, before the dark takes the rest. You are not as alone as the Wall wants you to believe."
"Sing, Aldous. The river-songs — the bad ones. Sing them loud, into the dark, toward the thin place. Someone on the other side needs to hear you do it. Sing, and don't stop."
"Hold on, voice. I'll come back. Don't fade yet."

nodes.speaker: a voice in the stone
nodes.text: *(The voice draws a long, shuddering breath, and something in it — some scattered thing — pulls suddenly together at the sound of the name.)* …*Aldous.* *(barely, then stronger)* *Aldous.* *Yes.* That's — *gods*, that's *MINE*, that's — the *reeds*, the *cold* *river*, the — I *sang*, didn't I, I *sang* *badly*, she always — *(a sound that is half a sob)* — *SHE.* The *warm* voice. The *'sorry* I was *late'* voice. That's — *(the voice steadies, gathering itself around the name like a hand around a coal)* — you've — traveler, you've *given* me *back* the *who.* I was *losing* it — the *Wall* *takes* the *who* *first*, the *name*, the *reeds*, all of it *sliding* *grey* — and you *reached* in and *said* *'Aldous'* and it all — it *caught.* *(fierce, frail)* I'm *Aldous.* I *cut* reeds. I *feared* the *dark* and I'm *IN* it and I'm *still* — I'm *still* *ME*, because a *cold* soul *said* my *NAME.* *(barely)* *Hold* it. *Whatever* you are. *Hold* the *name*, out *there*, where the *Wall* can't *reach* — because in *here* I'll *lose* it *again*, the *grey* *comes* *back*, but if *you* *hold* it, then I'm — then *Aldous* is *somewhere*, *safe*, even when I *can't* — *(the voice frays, then steadies)* — *thank* you. I didn't *know* a *name* could be *handed* *back.* I thought, once it *went*, it was — *gone.* You've *shown* me it can be *KEPT.* *Somewhere* *warm.* By *someone* who *bothers.*

nodes.speaker: a voice in the stone
nodes.text: *(You speak Pell's message through the thinness — every word — and on the other side the voice goes utterly still, and then breaks, the way only a soul that had given up breaks when the thing returns.)* …*Pell.* *(barely)* *Pell.* She — she *comes?* *Every* *night?* To the — to the *outside* of *this?* *(a sound of pure anguished joy)* I thought — I thought the *warm* voice was the *stone* being *cruel*, *inventing* a *love* to *taunt* me with, because *no* one — *who* would *come* to a *WALL*, *nightly*, for a *year*, for a *walled* *man?* *(the voice shakes)* …*Pell* would. *Of course* *Pell* would. *Forty* *years* and she — *(it weeps)* — and the *message* — *'you're not alone, I came back, I'm sorry I was late'* — *traveler*, tell her — *TELL* her — *(fierce, frail, urgent)* — tell her she was *NEVER* *late.* Tell her I *heard* her say *'don't* you *dare* go *anywhere'* and I — I *waited* as *long* as I *COULD*, I *swear* it, but the *fever* — and tell her *going* *alone* wasn't her *FAULT*, I was *never* *angry*, not for a *SECOND*, the *last* thing I *felt* wasn't *fear* of the *dark*, it was — it was *knowing* she'd be *cross* with *herself* *forever* and *wishing* I could *tell* her *don't* *be.* *(barely)* *That's* my *message* *back*, traveler. Not *'I* *forgive* you' — there was *never* *anything* to *forgive.* Just — *'you* *came* *back*, *Pell.* You *came* *back.* That's *all* I ever — that's *all* a man could — *you* *came* *back.'*

nodes.speaker: a voice in the stone
nodes.text: *(The voice presses close to the seam, and for a moment the fraying steadies, anchored by the impossible thing you've said.)* …You *walked* *out.* *(barely)* Of *this.* *(a long pause)* Then it's — it's *true*, what the *cold* ones are *whispered* to be. *(the voice wavers)* I'll not *ask* you to *carry* me, traveler. I can *feel* what's *left* of me — it's *not* enough to *carry.* The *Wall's* had *most* of *Aldous* *already*; what *crossed* with *you* would be a *handful* of *grey*, and I'd *not* *make* you *carry* a *handful* of *grey* and *call* it a *rescue.* *(steadier, gentler)* But you said the *next* thing. The *finding-each-other.* *(the voice warms)* *That* I'll *take.* *That* I'll take with *both* what's *left* of my *hands.* If you can make it so *Pell* and I *reach* each *other*, *once*, *true*, across this *stone* — so I can *tell* her *don't* *grieve*, and she can *tell* me *whatever* she's *carried* a *year* to *say* — then the *Wall* can have the *rest* of me *gladly.* *(fierce, fading)* I was *afraid* of *dying* *alone* in the *dark*, traveler. That was my *whole* *life's* *fear.* And you're telling me I *won't* — that there's a *warm* voice *right* *outside* and a *cold* soul who'll *carry* between us — *(barely)* — then I'm *not* *alone.* And if I'm *not* *alone*, the *dark* is just — *dark.* I can *do* *dark.* I *always* *could.* It was the *ALONE* I *couldn't* *do.* *Take* that *fear* *off* me, cold one, and you've done *more* than a *rescue.* You've done the *one* thing that *makes* the *un-making* *survivable*: you've made it *witnessed.*

nodes.speaker: a voice in the stone
nodes.text: *(There is a long silence from the other side of the stone — and then, cracked and faltering and unbearably off-key, the voice begins to sing.)* …*'The river don't care… what god you keep…'* *(it falters, finds it again)* …*'the river only… asks can you swim…'* *(stronger now, the bad old song rising)* — I *remember* it! Traveler, I *REMEMBER* the *whole* — *'so swim, you fool, swim… the bank's not far… and the reeds grow thick… where the dead girls are…'* *(a cracked laugh)* — it's a *FILTHY* song, the *third* verse is — *Pell* always *covered* the *children's* *ears* — *(it sings, louder, into the dark, toward the thin place)* — *'and the REEDS grow THICK…'* — *(and faintly, impossibly, from the other side of the Wall, through the thin place, you hear it: a woman's voice, choked with weeping, joining in)* — …*she's* — *(the voice breaks mid-note)* — *traveler*, she's — *she's* *SINGING* — *Pell's* *SINGING* the *third* *verse* — I can *HEAR* her — *(it can barely sing for weeping, but it sings)* — *forty* *years* and we're — we're *singing* the *bad* *song* *through* a *WALL* — *(barely)* — oh, this is — this is *better* than *getting* *out.* This is — *let* the *dark* *come* *now.* I'm *singing* with my *WIFE.*

nodes.speaker: a voice in the stone
nodes.text: *(The voice clings to the seam a moment longer, then eases back, gentler for having been heard at all.)* …You'll *come* *back.* *(barely)* They *say* that, sometimes. The *rare* *warm* things that *reach* in here. *'I'll* come *back.'* *(a pause)* I *used* to *not* *believe* it. *(softer)* But you're *cold*, and *real*, and you *answered*, and that's — that's *more* than the *stone's* given me in *longer* than I can *hold* in my — in whatever I have *left* to *hold* *with.* *(the voice steadies)* So I'll — I'll *wait.* I'm *good* at *waiting.* It's the *one* skill the *Wall* *teaches.* *(barely)* And cold one — if you *find* the *warm* voice out there — the *'sorry* I was *late'* one — *(the voice frays, then holds)* — *tell* her I'm — tell her *whoever* she's *waiting* for is *still* *here.* *Still* *enough* *here* to *wait* *back.* *That'll* — that'll *matter* to her. I don't *know* *how* I *know* that. But I *do.* Some things the *Wall* *can't* *take.* *Go* on. *Come* *back.* I'll be — *(faintly)* — I'll be *right* *here.* *Where* *else.*

variants.text: *(The weathered shade looks up from its knotted tally and regards your cold with a slow, knowing nod, the recognition of one student of thin places for another.)* …You feel them too. *(it gestures at the Wall)* The *thin* spots. Where the *stone* runs *close* and a *voice* might *carry.* *(it holds up the knotted string)* I've *mapped* them. *Forty* years of *kneeling* and *listening* and *marking* — *this* knot, the *seam* by the *third* *buttress*; *that* knot, the *crack* where the *old* *gate* was. *(it eyes you)* Most who *come* to the thin place come *once*, in *grief*, and *kneel* where the *grief* *takes* them, and *never* know they're at the *thickest* part of the *Wall*, *shouting* at *six* feet of *stone.* *(a dry sorrow)* I *show* them the *thin* spots. So their *grief* lands somewhere it might be *heard.* It's *all* an *old* surveyor of *sorrow* can *do* — *make* sure the *listening* happens where the *Wall* is *kindest.* Which is *nowhere* *kind.* But *thinnest.* *Thinnest* is the *best* *this* place *offers.*

nodes.speaker: the Reed-Knower
nodes.text: *(It runs the knotted string through its fingers, each knot a place it has learned the Wall goes thin.)* Ask, cold one. *(quietly)* I know this *Wall* the way the *Reed-Wife* knows the *river* — *every* thin spot, *every* seam, *every* place a *love* might *reach* a *hand's-breadth* *further* than the *stone* *wants.* It's a *grim* *expertise.* But it's *mine*, and it's *real*, and *tonight* it might be *useful.*

choices.text: "Goodwife Pell kneels at one spot every night. Is it the thinnest? If I'm to carry a voice across, I'd do it where the stone is kindest."
"Forty years mapping thin places — who were *you* listening for, when you started? Nobody surveys grief this carefully without a grief of their own."

*(one who crosses, to one who maps the crossings)* "You map where the Wall goes thin; I'm the one thing that can go *through* it. Between your knowledge and my cold, we're the closest thing this Wall has ever had to a door. Show me the thinnest seam, and stand witness — I mean to carry a love across it tonight, both ways, and I'd have the soul who mapped the way see it work, once, after forty years of only listening."
"Does it ever work, Reed-Knower? In forty years — has a voice ever truly reached the other side?"
"Keep your survey, Reed-Knower. I'll put it to use."

nodes.speaker: the Reed-Knower
nodes.text: *(It rises and crosses to where Pell kneels, running its hand along the Wall, and shakes its head slowly.)* …*Close.* *(it taps the stone a hand's-breadth left of Pell's ear)* But *not* the *thinnest.* *Here.* *This* *seam* — feel it? The *cold* comes *through* *sharper.* *(it traces it)* Poor *Pell* found a *thin* spot by *grief* alone, which is *more* than *most* manage — but the *thinnest* is *here*, where the *old* *gate* was *bricked*, the *mortar* *never* *set* *true.* *(it looks at you)* If you mean to *carry* a *voice* — or *be* one — *do* it *here.* A *whisper* through *this* seam carries *twice* as *far* as a *shout* through *hers.* *(quietly)* I never *told* her. *Couldn't.* How do you tell a *grieving* woman she's spent a *year* kneeling a *hand's-breadth* from where she'd have been *heard?* *(it meets your eyes)* But *you* can *fix* that, cold one, *without* the *cruelty* of the *telling* — just *bring* the *crossing* *here*, and let her *think* it was the *spot* she *chose.* *(a dry, kind sorrow)* Let her keep the *one* thing the *Wall* *left* her: the *belief* that she *found* the *place* *herself.*

nodes.speaker: the Reed-Knower
nodes.text: *(Its weathered face stills, and the knotted string goes quiet in its hands.)* …Who was *I* listening for. *(a long pause)* *Clever* of you to *ask.* *(it does not look at you)* My *daughter*, cold one. *Long* ago. *Walled* *young* — she was *barely* *grown*, *godless* like her *fool* of a *father*, and the *fever* took her, and the *Choir* took the *rest.* *(it runs a knot through its fingers)* I came to the *thin* place to *hear* her, the way *Pell* comes for *Aldous.* And I *knelt*, and I *listened*, and I *caught* — *nothing.* *Wrong* spot. *Six* feet of *stone.* *Months*, I *shouted* at *six* feet of *stone*, and she — on the *other* side, *fading*, *never* *hearing* — *(its voice roughens)* — she went *un-*heard, cold one. *Un*-witnessed. I was *RIGHT* *THERE* and the *Wall* was *thick* and she *died* the *second* death *alone* because her *fool* father *knelt* at the *wrong* *seam.* *(it steadies, terribly)* So I *learned* the thin places. *All* of them. *Too* *late* for *her.* But *not*, perhaps, too late for the *next* grieving fool with their *ear* to the *wrong* *stone.* *(it meets your eyes)* *That's* my *survey*, cold one. *Forty* years of *making* *sure* *no* one *else's* *love* lands on *deaf* *granite* the way *mine* did. It *won't* bring *her* *back.* *Nothing* will. But it's *turned* my *worst* *failure* into the *one* map this *Wall* has that's *made* of *mercy.* *Use* it. *Make* it *count.* *Make* *her* *un-heard* *death* *buy* *someone* *else's* *heard* one.

nodes.speaker: the Reed-Knower
nodes.text: *(It is quiet a long while, and the honesty it finally offers is hard-won.)* …Does it *work.* *(slowly)* *Truthfully?* *Rarely.* *(it turns the knots)* The *stone's* *thick* even at its *thinnest*, and the *walled* are *fading*, and *most* nights a *grieving* soul *whispers* its *heart* into a *seam* and gets *back* *only* the *cold.* I'll *not* *lie* and say I've *made* a *hundred* *reunions.* I've made — *(it considers)* — a *handful.* In *forty* years. *Handful.* *(but something kindles in it)* But *here's* the *thing*, cold one, the thing that *keeps* an *old* surveyor *kneeling*: a *handful* of *times*, a *voice* got *through.* A *walled* soul *heard* its *name*, *once* more, before the *grey.* A *widow* caught a *scrap* of a *bad* old *song* and *knew* her *husband* was *still* *singing.* *(it meets your eyes)* And *every* *one* of those *handful* was *worth* the *forty* years of *failures.* Because the *Wall's* whole *power*, cold one — the *thing* it *does* that's *worse* than *killing* — is to make the *dead* *believe* they're *alone* and *un-heard.* And *every* *single* *time* a *voice* gets *through*, *even* a *scrap*, *even* *once*, the *Wall* *loses* — because a soul that *knows* it's *heard* is a soul the *un-making* *can't* *fully* *finish.* *(fierce, frail)* So *does* it *work?* *Rarely.* But when it *works*, it *un-does* the *Wall's* *deepest* *cruelty* for *one* *soul* for *one* *moment* — and *you*, cold one, with your *crossing* — *you* could make it work *tonight*, *certain*, where I only ever managed it by *luck.* *Go.* *Make* the *rare* thing *sure.*

nodes.speaker: the Reed-Knower
nodes.text: *(It goes very still, and then, slowly, the weathered face does something it clearly hasn't done in a long time: it hopes.)* …The *closest* thing this *Wall* has *ever* *had* to a *door.* *(barely)* You and I. *(it grips the knotted string)* *Forty* years, cold one, I have *mapped* the *thin* places and *handed* the *map* to *grieving* fools and *watched* them *whisper* into *seams* and *mostly* get *back* the *cold* — and I have *never*, *not* *once*, been *able* to *carry* a *voice* *through* *myself.* I'm just the *surveyor.* I *find* the *thin* spots. I *can't* *cross* them. *(it meets your eyes, fierce)* But *you* can. And you're *asking* me to *show* you the *seam* and *stand* *witness* while you do the *thing* I've *spent* *forty* years *unable* to *do.* *(its voice shakes)* *Yes.* *Gods*, *yes.* *Come.* *This* seam — the *bricked* gate — *here.* *(it leads you, reverent)* And cold one — when you *carry* *Pell's* *voice* *through*, and *bring* *Aldous's* *back*, and the *two* of them *reach* each *other* across the *stone* that was *built* to *keep* them *apart* *forever* — *(barely)* — let me *watch.* Let an *old* fool who *knelt* at the *wrong* *seam* for his *own* *daughter* *watch* it *work*, *once*, *for* *someone.* It's the *closest* I'll ever *get* to *fixing* the *thing* I *couldn't* *fix.* And it might — *(its voice breaks)* — it might let me *finally* *believe* my *daughter* *could* have *been* *heard*, if *only* I'd *known* *then* what I *know* *now.* *That'd* be — that'd be a *mercy* *forty* years *late.* But a *mercy.* *Show* me it *works*, cold one. *Show* me it was *always* *possible.*

nodes.speaker: the Reed-Knower
nodes.text: *(It nods, and returns to its knotted survey, fingers moving over the record of forty years of thin places.)* …Put it to *use.* *(quietly)* That's *all* I've *ever* *wanted* for the *map* — that it be *used*, that the *kneeling* land *somewhere* it might be *heard.* *(it works the knots)* *Mind* the *thin* spots, cold one. And *mind* the *woman* at the *Wall.* She's *spent* a *year* of *nights* on the *one* thing the *Wall* *can't* *quite* *forbid* — *loving* *through* *stone* — and a soul like *that* deserves to find out, *once*, whether the *loving* *carried.* *(it glances up)* You're the *first* soul I've *met* who *might* *let* her *find* out *true.* *Don't* — *(a rare urgency)* — *don't* *waste* it. The *thin* place *doesn't* *stay* thin *forever.* The *Wall* *settles.* *Seams* *close.* And the *walled* *fade.* *Whatever* you *mean* to *do* *here* — *do* it while there's *still* a *voice* on the *other* *side* to *do* it *for.*
